description: Official strategy roster — named build targets with missions, roles, and victory definitions

layers.A: Core standalone killers — strong enough to trade alone
layers.B: Specialist enhancers — improve the portfolio, not necessarily top earners

targets.name: Opening Range Breakout Engine
targets.mission: Opening drive capture — exploit the first directional move after the opening range forms

victory_definition.summary: Robust breakout system that captures opening drive on 5m futures with prop-safe drawdown
conditions: Robust on 5m futures data across MES and MNQ minimum
Performs best during open session (09:30-11:00 ET)
Sufficient trade count (target 3+ trades/day in open session)
Low overfit signs — stable across 3+ month test windows
Prop-safe drawdown — max intraday DD stays under $2,000
Profit factor > 1.5 on primary asset
Positive expectancy per trade > $20

targets.notes: Primary breakout weapon. Should work across MES and MNQ at minimum.

targets.name: VWAP Mean Reversion Engine
targets.mission: Mean reversion / snapback capture — trade price returning to VWAP after overextension

victory_definition.summary: Stable snapback system with limited trend-day damage that complements breakout systems
conditions: Stable snapback behavior — consistent win rate on range/chop days
Limited trend-day damage — max losing day capped relative to avg winning day
Good expectancy — positive EV per trade > $15
Complements breakout systems — correlation < 0.3 with ALGO-CORE-ORB-001
Different failure mode from breakout (fails on chop, not trends)
Prop-safe — drawdown stays within trailing DD limits
Works on at least 2 assets (MES + one other)

targets.notes: Primary reversion weapon. Lucid v6 already has a VWAP reversion module to build from.

targets.name: ICT Liquidity Sweep Engine
targets.mission: Sweep / displacement / liquidity capture — exploit stop hunts and FVG fills

victory_definition.summary: Executable and testable sweep/displacement logic that survives cleaning without becoming discretionary
conditions: Executable and testable — fully codified, no discretionary judgment required
Sweep/displacement logic survives cleaning — core edge preserved after removing chart UI
Not overly discretionary — clear, programmable entry/exit rules
Distinct from trend and VWAP behavior — correlation < 0.3 with both
FVG detection is reliable and not repaint-prone
Positive expectancy despite lower frequency
Session-specific edge — measurably better in kill zones vs random hours

targets.notes: ICT concepts are harder to codify. Needs careful review for repaint risk and false signal rate.

targets.name: Trend Pullback Engine
targets.mission: Trend continuation via pullback entries — ride established trends with controlled entries

victory_definition.summary: Trend-following pullback system with trailing exits that captures extended moves on trend days
conditions: Strong performance on trend days — captures 50%+ of major daily moves
Controlled losses on chop days — quick stops, no bleed
Trailing exit captures extended runners beyond 1.5R
Robust across MES and MNQ at minimum
MTF confirmation reduces false entries in ranging markets
Prop-safe — intraday DD under $2,000, daily max loss controlled
Already has baseline: Lucid v6.3 trend pullback module

targets.notes: Lucid v6.3 trend pullback module is the baseline. PB module extracted to strategies/pb_trend/.

targets.name: PB Trend — MGC Short-Only
targets.mission: Gold-specific pullback trend-following on the short side

victory_definition.summary: Validated pullback short on MGC with PF > 1.5 and controlled drawdown
conditions: PF > 1.5 sustained over 50+ trades
Monthly consistency > 60%
Max drawdown < $500
Positive expectancy > $20/trade
Sample size > 50 trades for statistical confidence

risk_flags: Low frequency — many months with zero activity
targets.notes: First validated candidate from the lab. Extracted from Lucid v6.3 PB module. Needs extended data (4+ years) to confirm edge.

targets.name: Prop Master Stack
targets.mission: Best prop deployment engine — stack non-correlated algos for maximum daily profit with controlled drawdown

requirements.prop_rules: Lucid 100K — $4,000 EOD trailing DD, lock at +$3,100

victory_definition.summary: Combined stack with lower DD than any single component and improved payout probability under prop rules
conditions: Lower max drawdown than any single component alone
Improved payout probability vs best standalone algo
No dominant single-strategy dependence — removing any one component degrades but doesn't break the stack
Survives Monte Carlo simulation under prop rules (1000+ iterations)
Combined daily PnL variance is lower than individual components
Handles all market regimes — at least one component profitable in trend, range, and volatile days
Total trade frequency supports consistency rules

targets.notes: Built from the best of Layer A + Layer B. Only promoted after portfolio_tested status.
